brief_justification: Paper A studies critical points of a deterministic supergravity scalar potential and derives a stability condition for uplifting AdS vacua to Minkowski/de Sitter, while paper B computes large-deviation probabilities and extreme-value statistics of eigenvalues of random matrices via a Coulomb-gas/density-of-states analysis; there is no shared transferable machinery (deterministic potential-minimization/stability vs. probabilistic random-matrix large-deviation statistics), and no coherent role-for-role mapping between their elements.
